claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Let's start by understanding your situation. I have a few questions to ask you.

**First, could you tell me about the child (or children) you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?**

Specifically, I need to know:
- **How old is the child?** (or children, if more than one)
- **What is the child's name?** (so I can keep track if there are multiple children)

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to your situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking into claiming Child Benefit for my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 8 years old, she falls into the "under 16" category, which is great news for eligibility purposes.

Let me navigate to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is only 8 years old, she wouldn't be working 24+ hours per week (this check is mainly relevant for children aged 16 and over).

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

(For an 8-year-old, the answer would typically be "yes" - she's not in paid employment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, she's not working at all. She's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected for an 8-year-old in primary school. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in her own right?**

This means she is not personally claiming benefits such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

(For an 8-year-old, this would typically be "yes" - she wouldn't be receiving these benefits in her own name.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't receive any benefits in her own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's as expected. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is 8 years old and in primary school, she wouldn't be in an apprenticeship. Can you confirm she is **not** in any apprenticeship programme?

(This check is mainly relevant for older children, but I need to confirm to proceed.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's definitely not in an apprenticeship - she's only 8!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes complete sense! Let me move on to the next part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about your residency status. **Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and not claiming certain income-based benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You are a **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

**Do any of these apply to you?** For most people living in the UK, option 1 would apply.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through the Crown servant route.

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

This would include people such as:
- Members of the UK Armed Forces stationed overseas
- Diplomatic staff working at UK embassies or consulates abroad
- Civil servants posted overseas by the UK government

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries with specific arrangements?

If none of these apply and you simply live abroad, unfortunately you may not meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

**Does the Crown servant situation apply to you, or are you living abroad in other circumstances?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, I need to navigate to the outcome for not meeting the residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to deliver this news, but based on the assessment, I've reached a conclusion.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 8 | ❌ No | Claimant does not meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation

While Alex meets all the child-related criteria for Child Benefit (she's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you** as the claimant need to meet the residency requirements.

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria)
- Be a Crown servant living abroad
- Live in certain qualifying countries with special arrangements

### What You Can Do

1. **If your circumstances change** – If you move back to the UK or your situation changes (e.g., you become a Crown servant), you may become eligible at that point.

2. **Check for other support** – Depending on where you're living, there may be family benefit schemes in your country of residence.

3. **Get further advice** – If you think your situation might qualify you (for example, if you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme), you can contact HMRC directly for guidance:
   - Visit: [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)
   - Call the Child Benefit helpline: 0300 200 3100

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know or any other questions I can help with?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and not in an apprenticeship - so she meets all the child-related qualifying criteria. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not fall into any of the other qualifying residency categories. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While their daughter Alex meets all the qualifying criteria for a child, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad, or having settled/pre-settled status) to claim Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and not in an apprenticeship - so she meets all the child-related qualifying criteria. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not fall into any of the other qualifying residency categories. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While their daughter Alex meets all the qualifying criteria for a child, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad, or having settled/pre-settled status) to claim Child Benefit.
